changes_summary: Revised the plan to explicitly migrate `LightboxShell` through a sanctioned overlay wrapper path, preserve and centralize marker-based `container` resolution, and include the concrete helper callers and type-only raw import cleanup needed to satisfy the no-raw-import and single-source-of-truth goals.

success_criteria.criterion: `src/shared/state/overlayStack.ts` exists, exports JSDoc-documented public actions/selectors for overlay registration, topmost queries, and stack-derived layering, and is the only automated source of truth for modal body pointer-events and focus restoration.

No raw `@base-ui/react/(dialog|popover|select|menu|tooltip|alert-dialog|context-menu|popup|preview-card)` imports remain anywhere under `src/` outside `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/`, including type-only imports that would fail the invariant grep.

`LightboxShell.tsx` no longer imports `@base-ui/react/dialog` directly and instead consumes a sanctioned overlay-aware wrapper path under `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/` that preserves its current modal, dismissal-cancellation, and custom event-handler contract.
The existing Base UI `preview-card` portal wrapper is migrated into the centralized overlay ownership layer so no in-repo Base UI portal primitive with stack-relevant ownership remains outside M2.

DOM/z-index precedence helpers are retired for migrated overlay flows: `uiLayers.ts`, lightbox interaction/navigation hooks, and `LoraSelectorModal` no longer determine topmost state by querying DOM markers and comparing computed z-index values.

Helper-based `container` resolution is centralized and preserved: the known helper callers in `CommunityLorasTab.tsx`, `MyLorasTab.tsx`, and `sharedSections.tsx` are migrated to the shared helper, and the relevant overlay wrappers still expose compatible `data-dialog-content` / `data-lightbox-popup` markers during M2.

Caller-side layering escape hatches for migrated overlay primitives are removed or neutralized: no remaining `zIndexBase`, overlay z-index class overrides, or equivalent caller-owned numeric layering controls are needed for Dialog, Select, Popover, Menu, Tooltip, AlertDialog, or HoverCard flows.
Integration tests cover `open A -> open B -> close A -> close B` for both Dialog+Popover and Menu+Dialog, and assert `document.body.style.pointerEvents === ''` after full close.

Tests that previously encoded the old DOM-z-index ordering strategy are updated or replaced so the suite validates the new stack-based precedence model instead, including the lightbox precedence path.
`npm test` passes after the overlay migration.
Public wrapper entrypoints remain import-compatible for existing consumers where possible, while removing only the layering-specific escape hatches that conflict with central ownership.
The overlay stack implementation remains a small, dedicated ordered-store abstraction rather than absorbing unrelated persistence or styling responsibilities.

Deferred manual checks pass in a real app session: nested LoRA modal plus Model Select closes cleanly, drag interactions do not spuriously dismiss overlays, and focus trap / Escape / restore behavior feels correct.

assumptions: The critique indicates missing integration detail, not a wrong root-cause hypothesis: the milestone should still centralize Base UI portal ownership and overlay ordering rather than pivot to a different subsystem.
`LightboxShell.tsx` is in scope because it is a live raw Base UI dialog owner; migrating it is required to satisfy the raw-import invariant, not optional scope growth.

Existing marker selectors such as `[data-dialog-content]` and `[data-lightbox-popup]` remain compatibility contracts for this milestone because helper-based `container` resolution and CSS still depend on them.
A dedicated lightbox-specific wrapper under `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/` is the narrowest way to preserve LightboxShell's specialized Base UI contract while centralizing raw portal ownership.

Automated verification remains limited to shell and jsdom/Vitest. Any verification that requires a running browser session or manual interaction is deferred-human and should be reported as skipped, not blocked.
